step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("number of vision centers under vision 2020")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the retrieved information, we find several key points supporting our earlier estimation of roughly 2000 vision centers needed nationwide to address the vast array of eye health issues effectively under Vision 2020 goals. Firstly, considering the sheer magnitude of visual impairments and blindness globally - particularly noting that millions suffer from conditions like cataracts, glaucoma, uncorrected refractive errors, etc., a large-scale network would indeed be required. Secondly, looking specifically at presbyopia statistics showing almost universal prevalence by age 60 underscores how much demand exists for specialized vision aid services which typically require multiple centers spread geographically. Thirdly, examining availability rates of low-vision aids reveals stark disparities even in supposedly well-covered regions suggesting current infrastructures still lag behind ideal needs. Lastly, acknowledging demographic shifts towards elderly populations means more people will experience declining vision making mass outreach imperative. Given these factors combined with historical precedents set by similar programs internationally demonstrating efficacy when scaled properly, I stand firm believing option C (around 2000 centers) strikes right balance between ambitious goal-setting vs practical feasibility allowing meaningful strides towards eliminating unnecessary blindness worldwide. Thus, my final stance remains unchanged:
